A Staten Island house fire injured four firefighters and five residents, while NYC's subway service faced significant disruptions post-blizzard, leading to school closures and emergency responses across the city. Additionally, a former Bronx councilman and wedding guests were stranded in Mexico amid cartel unrest.

### Risks & Alerts

- Four firefighters and five residents injured in Staten Island house fire; over 141 emergency personnel responded. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Former Bronx City Councilman Andy King and 25 wedding party guests stranded in Mexico due to cartel unrest. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMixAFBVV95cUxOMFhtWmxhSVdTQkNiSThjOE0weExvQTgtZzR1d0Z3aXdnYWJ3cDVjeVVIQWh6ZHkxQnBLdmVTYUdzMUJTNC1lLVFUeENOV3BkaEw3MkFCVWNVbFd0LVk5SUFHQnAtNnhFUVlXdzFoSkMzUlJaelNhd05GSXp6Z1pxQXdfeVc5dVQ2QUdraGwtLVpfNW5GTi1rdFRYNmQzWW83U3dRa2FKeVdXdHFhRUpKQWU5Sk4tLWNlUjlVMmFIMy1sMWM1?oc=5)
- NYC subway service disrupted as riders return following blizzard; expect delays. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMilwFBVV95cUxNOUgzcXE0ZGcyUDdqcmtjS3R4aFE0VkVfLTFQay1GV1I4VGhHQ0ZlWUI0RnZMdDBFRUV1LUxkQlE1VWxzODV0aHZFXzVjY3Q5NDNJSmstMFlWOGU0Z08tbGlrT29OZ0dmTHVzOVFETnZXcnpOM3loT0g1RkR0aEo1eTdyTmF2T0FzOHVjenRhbFhKLVBIcmhJ?oc=5)
- Yonkers schools closed for a third day due to heavy snow from the blizzard. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Snowstorm caused numerous trees to fall across NYC, posing hazards in various neighborhoods. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiiwFBVV95cUxOVEFxSUJ1ZEs0M0lsRmptSmduajZtM2JIVEZJaFc2SnQxQTFEeWhUNVRTWjFudm5QblRzOWp2R3BjSlNZcGxZYkt2SEk3ZjA5X2wwWTM0YlVxeTgwZmRBSmUzOVZWdUZla1Y0bHJpckgxWUZDTG1JeTRhN2VjT2ZJVHcwZGdfZDQ2RHc0?oc=5)
- Metro-North line service limited due to a car on the tracks; check for updates. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Customers evacuated from Houlihan’s after reports of cracking noises from heavy snow on the roof. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- MTA threatens to sue Trump administration over frozen 2nd Avenue subway funds. ### Opportunities & Welfare

- Mounting eviction cases indicate NYC's 'affordable' housing is becoming increasingly unaffordable.
